title: Downstream config surfaces — skills, context, session, rate limit
purpose: Wire the remaining settings fields into their consumers: skill search paths, context search paths, session history capacity, and provider rate limit. These are the last-mile connections that make the config system end-to-end functional. After this brief, every settings field defined in NC-002 has a consumer.
task: Wire skills.search_paths from merged settings into the SkillSearchPaths extension on ToolContext. Wire context.search_paths into a discoverable extension. Wire session.history_capacity into REPL history construction. Wire provider.rate_limit into the OpenAI provider rate limiter. Verify all linting and quality gates pass.

requirements.title: Skill search paths from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain skills.search_paths, THE SYSTEM SHALL include those directories in the SkillSearchPaths extension installed on the shared ToolContext, alongside the default scan directories.
acceptance: Settings skills.search_paths values are prepended to the SkillSearchPaths vec
Default scan directories (.norn/skills/, ~/.norn/skills/) are still included
Settings paths are resolved relative to the working directory
Test: settings skill paths appear in SkillSearchPaths extension

requirements.title: Context search paths from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain context.search_paths, THE SYSTEM SHALL make them available as a discoverable ToolContext extension so the context cluster can read them.
acceptance: ContextSearchPaths struct defined (newtype over Vec<PathBuf>)
ContextSearchPaths installed on the shared ToolContext when settings provide context.search_paths
Settings paths are resolved relative to the working directory
Test: settings context paths accessible via ToolContext extension

requirements.title: Session history capacity from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain session.history_capacity, THE SYSTEM SHALL use that value for REPL history construction instead of the hardcoded HISTORY_CAPACITY constant.
acceptance: REPL history construction reads session.history_capacity from merged settings
Absent setting falls back to the existing HISTORY_CAPACITY constant
Test: settings history_capacity overrides the default

requirements.title: Provider rate limit from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain provider.rate_limit, THE SYSTEM SHALL use that value for the OpenAI provider rate limiter permits-per-minute instead of the hardcoded DEFAULT_PERMITS_PER_INTERVAL constant. The actual default value is a Tom-decision.
acceptance: OpenAI provider construction reads rate_limit from merged settings or ProviderConfigOverrides
Absent setting falls back to the existing DEFAULT_PERMITS_PER_INTERVAL constant
The default constant value is unchanged (pending Tom's decision on whether to adjust)
Test: settings rate_limit overrides the default

requirements.title: Linting and quality verification
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL pass all linting gates across the workspace after all NC-001 through NC-005 changes.
acceptance: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean
cargo fmt --check passes clean
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
No .unwrap() or .expect() in norn crate library code
No TODO, FIXME, or HACK markers in production code

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement skill loading or activation — that is the skills cluster
SHALL NOT implement context injection or context file parsing — that is the context cluster
SHALL NOT implement hook execution — that is the hooks cluster
SHALL NOT modify NornSettings types or merge logic — those were NC-002 and NC-003
SHALL NOT change the rate limit default value without Tom's approval (Tom-decision)
verification: SkillSearchPaths includes settings paths (verified by test)
ContextSearchPaths extension installed when settings provide paths (verified by test)
REPL history respects settings capacity (verified by test)
Rate limiter respects settings rate_limit (verified by test)
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
grep -r 'TODO\|FIXME\|HACK\|unimplemented!' crates/norn/src/ crates/norn-cli/src/ returns no matches in production code
